title: Abort: err-code vs `PyErr` desync in `handle_channel_error` (`_interpchannelsmodule.c:398` / `:443`)
description: The _interpchannels create path threads a hand-rolled int error code in parallel with PyErr; handle_channel_error asserts they agree (err==0 => !PyErr_Occurred() at L398; unhandled err<0 => PyErr_Occurred() at L443). Under OOM (L398 form) newchannelid() fails (-1, MemoryError pending) and the cleanup handle_channel_error(channel_destroy()==0) runs with that MemoryError still pending; or (L443 form) channel_create() returns -1 with no exception set. Either aborts.
notes: Two faces of one defect (int error code vs PyErr desync in the _interpchannels create path). L398 form (minimal repro, gdb-verified): newchannelid (channelsmod_create L2948) fails under OOM -> returns -1 with MemoryError pending; first handle_channel_error(-1) at L2951 passes (err<0 + exc set, L443 holds) and enters the cleanup block; channel_destroy (L2953) returns 0 without clearing the MemoryError; the second handle_channel_error(0) at L2954 trips assert(!PyErr_Occurred()) at L398. (NOT 'newchannelid returns 0 with pending' -- an earlier draft was wrong; the only newchannelid-returns-0 path does no allocation.) L443 form (vehicle): channel_create (L2938) returns -1 at L2940 without setting an exception -> assert(PyErr_Occurred()) at L443. Fix: (L443) channel_create must set an exception on every <0 return; (L398) the channelsmod_create cleanup must preserve/restore the pending exception across channel_destroy (PyErr_Get/SetRaisedException) or skip the second handle_channel_error once the first established the error. Debug-only asserts (NDEBUG strips them). Distinct from OOM-0014 (channelsmod__channel_id, :3487). Exception-state-under-OOM family. Minimal repro reproduces deterministically (L398 form). One of 3 deferred abort singletons triaged 2026-06-18.
